channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1096)—Answers for Thursday, June 11
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 11)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • DEVIL
  • STRIP
  • PAPAL
  • BRONCHO
  • VENO
  • BRASS BAND
  • UCONN
  • RHINO
  • CARDIO
  • TROUPER
  • VIKING HELMET
  • BALANCE
  • ELLE
  • WEIGHTS
  • FORERUNNER
  • STRETCHING

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – What you might do in a gym
  • Green – Things that have horns
  • Blue – Names of popular SUVs but not correctly
  • Purple – Almost the names of payment apps

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 11!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 11 (#1096)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BALANCE, CARDIO, STRETCHING, WEIGHTS
  • Green – BRASS BAND, DEVIL, RHINO, VIKING HELMET
  • Blue – BRONCHO, FORERUNNER, TROUPER, UCONN
  • Purple – ELLE, PAPAL, STRIP, VENO

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-june-11-109/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bethesda to Reveal Elder Scrolls 6 ‘At the Right Time,’ Eight Years After First Trailer
item.content:encoded:
XBOX’s Chief Content Officer has recently commented on Elder Scrolls 6’s reveal, and it seems to be shaping up well, but a reveal doesn’t seem close.

We already know that Elder Scrolls 6’s release is a long way off, as revealed by Todd Howard back in 2025. However, players have been craving for new information on the next installment of the highly revered Bethesda series for 8 years now. It seems that the developers won’t reveal it until it’s the right time.

In a recent interview with Variety, Matt Booty, XBOX CCO, was asked about the reveal of Elder Scrolls 6 and how fans anticipated its announcement at the Xbox Games Showcase 2026. Booty mentioned that he had visited Bethesda, where he met Todd Howard and observed Elder Scrolls being played. He said, “It looks amazing, and it’s coming along well.” Regarding the announcement, he noted that the team will “make sure to announce it and really reveal it at the right time.”

It has been 8 years since the initial announcement, and the players have close to no information about the upcoming title. The announcement trailer featured nothing but a vast expanse of land filled with scenic mountains, a whole lot of greenery, a large body of water, and more. The trailer ended with the Elder Scrolls 6 title being shown. That’s all we have to go on for now, and the game is powered by Creation Engine 3.

In other news, in a behind-closed-doors preview of Infestations, Bethesda explained why the new Fallout 76 update is so important. Additionally, former Bethesda veteran Pete Hines has explained why he left the company after a quarter of a century. What are your thoughts on Elder Scrolls 6 reveal not being rushed 8 years after the initial announcement? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Ubisoft’s restructuring impacting 380 employees. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Ubisoft Announces Approximately 380 Employees Could be Impacted by Restructuring Changes
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has announced to its employees that a new wave of restructuring could lead to approximately 380 employees being impacted.

In the announcement, which was posted to Ubisoft’s internal communications channel, the update said,

“Over the past months. Ubisoft has been evolving its organization to simplify how it operates, reduce its cost base. and strengthen the company for the long term. These decisions also reflect adjustments to the level of activity following recent portfolio reviews.

As part of this effort, we have made the following announcements today regarding our teams and operations.

In Production. difficult decisions were made to close our studios in Winnipeg and Belgrade, and we have initiated consultations regarding a proposed restructuring of our Barcelona studio with a refocus of activities on Rainbow Six subject to employee representative consultations.

Changes are also underway across the global Publishing organization, to adapt how teams are structured and deployed, while maintaining a strong presence in key markets.

Pending consultations, these decisions mean that up to 380 employees could be impacted.

These changes do not reflect the talent, commitment, or contributions of the people affected.

Local management teams are engaging directly with employees to provide information, answer questions, and ensure appropriate support throughout the process.”

Insider Gaming understands that dozens of employees were laid off as part of the global publishing organization, which was based in San Francisco.

At the time of writing, the breakdown of those affected is believed to be:

  • Ubisoft Winnipeg, 65 employees laid off, and the studio closed
  • Ubisoft Belgrade, approximately 100 employees were laid off, and the studio closed
  • Ubisoft Barcelona, 51 employees laid off
  • Rainbow Six Siege, 120 employees ramped off
  • Ubisoft San Francisco, undisclosed number of employees laid off

In other news, read about Roblox looking to prevent child sexual abuse lawsuits from going to court. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Dozens of Employees Laid Off at Ubisoft San Francisco Offices
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has laid off dozens of employees at Ubisoft’s San Francisco offices.

The announcement was made today in an internal meeting with staff, sources say, where it was said that the changes were being made as a part of the latest ongoing cost-restructuring efforts.

Ubisoft San Francisco’s development studio had already closed in late 2024, but it remained open to Ubisoft employees in areas such as IT, marketing, and so on.

The announcement is a part of yet another broader bloodbath at Ubisoft today, with two studios, Winnipeg and Belgrade, being closed, and other studios seeing dozens of employees laid off.

The exact number of those laid off is said to be somewhere between 50-100, but an exact number is not yet known.

Insider Gaming has reached out to Ubisoft for comment; should one be received, we will update this article.

This is a developing story and will be updated shortly.

In other news, read about Roblox looking to prevent child sexual abuse lawsuits from going to court. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: New Crash Bandicoot Game is in Development, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
It appears that a new game in the Crash Bandicoot franchise is currently in development, according to a recent claim.

Players have been waiting for quite a bit of time for a new installment to the franchise; the last mainline entry was back in 2020 with Crash Bandicoot 4: It’s About Time, and the latest entry was Crash Team Rumble, a multiplayer spinoff. It seems like another Crash Bandicoot is in the oven.

Spyro Leaker Reveals New Crash Bandicoot Game is in Development

The latest rumor about a new Crash Bandicoot game comes from a user on X (formerly Twitter) named noarmsandnolegs. This leaker has previously shared accurate details about the upcoming Spyro game before it was officially announced. In their latest post, they mentioned that “a new Crash Bandicoot game is scheduled too,” although it appears that the developers are not planning to make any announcements about it at this time. Readers should take this information with a grain of salt.

Crash Bandicoot 4: It’s About Time was a time-shattered adventure where players received new abilities, more playable characters, alternate dimensions, and a whole lot more as Crash and Coco work towards saving the multiverse. Crash Rumble is a 4v4 team-based competition where iconic heroes and villains from the Crash universe battle to capture more Wumpa fruit than their opponents to claim victory.

In other news, Activision has filed a new trademark for Crash Bandicoot, specifically revolving around motion pictures and television programs. Additionally, an animated Crash Bandicoot series by Netflix and WildBrain Studios is reportedly quietly in the works. What are your thoughts on a new Crash Bandicoot game being in development? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about PlayStation Plus Game Catalog titles for June 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
